revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 松原神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 鹿児島県鹿児島市松原町3-35 |祭神 = 島津貴久(靖国崇勲彦命) |社格 = 県社 |創建 = 明治2年（1869年） |本殿 = |別名 = 大中様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月5日・10月5日 |神事 = }}
'''松原神社'''（まつばらじんじゃ）は[[鹿児島県]][[鹿児島市]]にある[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[島津氏]]15代当主・[[島津貴久]]を主祭神として、相殿に天之御中主神・高御産巣日神・神産巣日神・天照皇大神・豊受大明神を祀る。
== 歴史 == [[弘治 (日本)|弘治]]3年（[[1567年]]）、[[島津氏]]の中興の祖である[[島津貴久]]により[[福昌寺 (鹿児島市)|福昌寺]]の末寺として建立された松原山[[南林寺 (鹿児島市)]]を前身とする。貴久の死後はその跡を継いだ[[島津義久]]によって菩提寺と定められ、以後歴代の薩摩藩主より手厚い保護を受けたが、[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]])、[[廃仏毀釈]]の煽りを受けて廃寺。跡地に南林寺の山号に因んで松原神社が創建され、これをもって神社の創始とする。 貴久の350年祭にあたる[[大正]]9年（[[1920年]])には県社に列し、現在でも鹿児島の繁華街近くの神社として、祭日及び年末年始は多くの参拝者で賑わう。
== 境内社 == *稲荷神社 *厳島神社
== 歯の神 == 貴久の家臣であった[[平田純貞]]は、貴久の没後に自ら釘付けとなり船出して殉死した。その際に3日3晩にわたって読経の声と歯軋りの音が聞こえたことから歯の神として称えられるようになり貴久の傍らに葬られた。現在でも全国虫歯予防デーには県の歯科医師会主催の抜歯供養が斎行される。
== 参考 == かごしま暦（鹿児島県神社庁編）
{{DEFAULTSORT:まつはらしんしや}} [[Category:鹿児島市の神社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 松原神社 |画像 = [[File:Matsubara-shrine.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 鹿児島県鹿児島市松原町3番35号 |祭神 = 島津貴久(靖国崇勲彦命) |社格 = 県社 |創建 = 明治2年（1869年） |本殿 = |別名 = 大中様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月5日・10月5日 |神事 = }}
'''松原神社'''（まつばらじんじゃ）は[[鹿児島県]][[鹿児島市]][[松原町 (鹿児島市)|松原町]]にある[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[島津氏]]15代当主・[[島津貴久]]を主祭神として、相殿に天之御中主神・高御産巣日神・神産巣日神・天照皇大神・豊受大明神を祀る。
== 歴史 == [[弘治 (日本)|弘治]]3年（[[1567年]]）、[[島津氏]]の中興の祖である[[島津貴久]]により[[福昌寺 (鹿児島市)|福昌寺]]の末寺として建立された松原山[[南林寺 (鹿児島市)]]を前身とする。貴久の死後はその跡を継いだ[[島津義久]]によって菩提寺と定められ、以後歴代の薩摩藩主より手厚い保護を受けたが、[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]])、[[廃仏毀釈]]の煽りを受けて廃寺。跡地に南林寺の山号に因んで松原神社が創建され、これをもって神社の創始とする。
貴久の350年祭にあたる[[大正]]9年（[[1920年]])には県社に列し、現在でも鹿児島の繁華街近くの神社として、祭日及び年末年始は多くの参拝者で賑わう。
== 境内社 == *稲荷神社 *厳島神社
== 歯の神 == 貴久の家臣であった[[平田純貞]]は、貴久の没後に自ら釘付けとなり船出して殉死した。その際に3日3晩にわたって読経の声と歯軋りの音が聞こえたことから歯の神として称えられるようになり貴久の傍らに葬られた。現在でも全国虫歯予防デーには県の歯科医師会主催の抜歯供養が斎行される。
== 参考文献 == *かごしま暦（鹿児島県神社庁編）
{{DEFAULTSORT:まつはらしんしや}} [[Category:鹿児島市の神社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 松原神社 |画像 = [[File:Matsubara-shrine.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 鹿児島県鹿児島市松原町3番35号 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|35|11.2|N|130|33|26.8|E|scale:25000|name=松原神社}} |祭神 = 靖國崇勲彦命（島津貴久公） |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = 明治2年（1869年） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 大中様（だいつさぁ） |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月5日・10月5日 |神事 = }}
'''松原神社'''（まつばらじんじゃ）は[[鹿児島県]][[鹿児島市]][[松原町 (鹿児島市)|松原町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[島津氏]]15代当主・[[島津貴久]]を'''靖國崇勲彦命'''（くにむけいさたかひこのみこと）または島津貴久公と称して主祭神とし、[[相殿]]に[[天之御中主神]]・[[高御産巣日神]]・[[神産巣日神]]・[[天照皇大神]]・[[豊宇気毘売神|豊受大明神]]・[[平田純貞]]を祀る。 ;歯の神 相殿に祀られる平田純貞は祭神の家臣で、祭神没後に自ら釘付けとなり船出して殉死するが、その際に3日3晩にわたって読経の声と歯軋りの音が聞こえたことから歯の神として称えられるようになり祭神の傍らに葬られた。現在でも[[虫歯予防デー|全国虫歯予防デー]]には県の歯科医師会主催の抜歯供養が斎行される。
== 歴史 == {{Seealso|南林寺 (鹿児島市)}} [[弘治 (日本)|弘治]]3年（[[1567年]]）、[[島津氏]]の中興の祖である祭神により[[福昌寺 (鹿児島市)|福昌寺]]の末寺として建立された[[南林寺 (鹿児島市)|松原山南林寺]]を前身とする。祭神歿後はその跡を継いだ[[島津義久]]によって[[菩提寺]]と定められ、以後歴代の[[薩摩藩]]藩主より手厚い保護を受けたが、[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）、[[廃仏毀釈]]の煽りを受けて廃寺。跡地に南林寺の山号に因んで当神社が創祀されて[[郷社]]に列し、祭神歿後の350年祭にあたる[[大正]]9年（[[1920年]]）には[[県社]]に昇格した。現在でも鹿児島の繁華街近くの神社として、祭日及び年末年始は多くの参拝者で賑わう。
== 境内社 == *稲荷神社（[[稲荷大神]]（いなりのおおかみ）） *厳島神社（[[宗像三女神|市杵島姫命（いちきしまひめのみこと）・田心姫命（たごりひめのみこと）・湍津姫命（たぎつひめのみこと）]]）
== 参考文献 == *鹿児島県神社庁編『かごしま暦』、鹿児島県神社庁
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-907.html 松原神社]（鹿児島県神社庁）

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Matsubara Shrine | image = [[File:Matsubara-shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Kagoshima Prefecture]][[Kagoshima City]][[Matsubara-cho (Kagoshima City)|Matsubara-cho]] 3-35 | ISO = JP-46 | latitude_deg = 31 | type_min = 35 | type_sec = 11.2 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 33 | longitude_sec = 26.8 | deity = Yasukuni Sukunehiko no Mikoto (Shimazu Takahisa) | shrine_rank = Former Prefectural Shrine | founded = Meiji 2 (1869) | main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | other_names = Daitsusa | annual_festivals = April 5, October 5 | map = Japan Kagoshima city central#Japan Kagoshima city }}
'''Matsubara Shrine''' (松原神社, Matsubara Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Matsubara-cho (Kagoshima City)|Matsubara-cho]], [[Kagoshima City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.[[ja:松原神社 (鹿児島市)]]
== Deities == The main deity is Yasukuni Sukunehiko no Mikoto, also known as [[Shimazu Takahisa]], the 15th head of the [[Shimazu clan]]. The shrine also enshrines the following deities in the same hall: * [[Amenominakanushi]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]] * [[Hirata Sumitada]]
; Tooth God Hirata Sumitada, a retainer of the main deity, is also enshrined. After the main deity's death, Hirata Sumitada became a nail-bound martyr. During his martyrdom, chanting and tooth-grinding sounds were heard for three days and nights, leading to his veneration as the god of teeth. He was buried next to the main deity. Even today, during the [[National Cavity Prevention Day]], the Kagoshima Dental Association holds an exodontia memorial service at the shrine.
== History == {{Seealso|Nanrinji (Kagoshima City)}} In [[Koji (Japan)|Koji]] 3 (1567), [[Shimazu Takahisa]], the reviver of the Shimazu clan, established Matsubarayama Nanrinji, a branch temple of [[Fukushoji]]. After the deity's death, [[Shimazu Yoshihisa]] designated it as the family temple, receiving significant protection from successive [[Satsuma Domain]] lords. However, in Meiji 2 (1869), during the [[Haibutsu Kishaku]] movement, the temple was abolished. The shrine was then established on the site, taking the mountain name of Nanrinji. It became a village shrine, and in [[Taisho]] 9 (1920), on the 350th anniversary of the deity's death, it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. Even today, it is bustling with many worshippers during festivals and the New Year holidays, located near the busy downtown area of Kagoshima.
== Sub-Shrines == * [[Inari Shrine]] ([[Inari Okami]]) * [[Itsukushima Shrine]] ([[Ichikishimahime no Mikoto]], [[Tagorihime]], [[Tagitsuhime no Mikoto]])
== References == * Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency, "Kagoshima Calendar," Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency.

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Matsubara Shrine | image = [[File:Matsubara-shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Kagoshima Prefecture]][[Kagoshima City]][[Matsubara-cho (Kagoshima City)|Matsubara-cho]] 3-35 | ISO = JP-46 | latitude_deg = 31 | type_min = 35 | type_sec = 11.2 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 33 | longitude_sec = 26.8 | deity = Yasukuni Sukunehiko no Mikoto (Shimazu Takahisa) | shrine_rank = Former Prefectural Shrine | founded = Meiji 2 (1869) | main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | other_names = Daitsusa | annual_festivals = April 5, October 5 | map = Japan Kagoshima city central#Japan Kagoshima city }}
'''Matsubara Shrine''' (松原神社, Matsubara Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Matsubara-cho (Kagoshima City)|Matsubara-cho]], [[Kagoshima City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.[[ja:松原神社 (鹿児島市)]]
== Deities == The main deity is Yasukuni Sukunehiko no Mikoto, also known as [[Shimazu Takahisa]], the 15th head of the [[Shimazu clan]]. The shrine also enshrines the following deities in the same hall: * [[Amenominakanushi]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]] * [[Hirata Sumitada]]
; Tooth God Hirata Sumitada, a retainer of the main deity, is also enshrined. After the main deity's death, Hirata Sumitada became a nail-bound martyr. During his martyrdom, chanting and tooth-grinding sounds were heard for three days and nights, leading to his veneration as the god of teeth. He was buried next to the main deity. Even today, during the [[National Cavity Prevention Day]], the Kagoshima Dental Association holds an exodontia memorial service at the shrine.
== History == {{Seealso|Nanrinji (Kagoshima City)}} In [[Koji (Japan)|Koji]] 3 (1567), [[Shimazu Takahisa]], the reviver of the Shimazu clan, established Matsubarayama Nanrinji, a branch temple of [[Fukushoji]]. After the deity's death, [[Shimazu Yoshihisa]] designated it as the family temple, receiving significant protection from successive [[Satsuma Domain]] lords. However, in Meiji 2 (1869), during the [[Haibutsu Kishaku]] movement, the temple was abolished. The shrine was then established on the site, taking the mountain name of Nanrinji. It became a village shrine, and in [[Taisho]] 9 (1920), on the 350th anniversary of the deity's death, it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. Even today, it is bustling with many worshippers during festivals and the New Year holidays, located near the busy downtown area of Kagoshima.
== Sub-Shrines == * [[Inari Shrine]] ([[Inari Okami]]) * [[Itsukushima Shrine]] ([[Ichikishimahime no Mikoto]], [[Tagorihime]], [[Tagitsuhime no Mikoto]])
== References == * Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency, "Kagoshima Calendar," Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency.

== Deities == The main deity is Yasukuni Sukunehiko no Mikoto, also known as [[Shimazu Takahisa]], the 15th head of the [[Shimazu clan]]. The shrine also enshrines the following deities in the same hall: * [[Amenominakanushi]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]] * [[Hirata Sumitada]]
; Tooth God Hirata Sumitada, a retainer of the main deity, is also enshrined. After the main deity's death, Hirata Sumitada became a nail-bound martyr. During his martyrdom, chanting and tooth-grinding sounds were heard for three days and nights, leading to his veneration as the god of teeth. He was buried next to the main deity. Even today, during the [[National Cavity Prevention Day]], the Kagoshima Dental Association holds an exodontia memorial service at the shrine.
== History == {{Seealso|Nanrinji (Kagoshima City)}} In [[Koji (Japan)|Koji]] 3 (1567), [[Shimazu Takahisa]], the reviver of the Shimazu clan, established Matsubarayama Nanrinji, a branch temple of [[Fukushoji]]. After the deity's death, [[Shimazu Yoshihisa]] designated it as the family temple, receiving significant protection from successive [[Satsuma Domain]] lords. However, in Meiji 2 (1869), during the [[Haibutsu Kishaku]] movement, the temple was abolished. The shrine was then established on the site, taking the mountain name of Nanrinji. It became a village shrine, and in [[Taisho]] 9 (1920), on the 350th anniversary of the deity's death, it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. Even today, it is bustling with many worshippers during festivals and the New Year holidays, located near the busy downtown area of Kagoshima.
== Sub-Shrines == * [[Inari Shrine]] ([[Inari Okami]]) * [[Itsukushima Shrine]] ([[Ichikishimahime no Mikoto]], [[Tagorihime]], [[Tagitsuhime no Mikoto]])
== References == * Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency, "Kagoshima Calendar," Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== External Links == * [https://matsubarajinja.com Matsubara Shrine Official Website] * {{wayback|url=http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-907.html |title=Matsubara Shrine |date=2015-01-06}} (Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency)

Even today, during the [[National Cavity Prevention Day]], the Kagoshima Dental Association holds an exodontia memorial service at the shrine.

After the deity's death, [[Shimazu Yoshihisa]] designated it as the family temple, receiving significant protection from successive [[Satsuma Domain]] lords. However, in Meiji 2 (1869), during the [[Haibutsu Kishaku]] movement, the temple was abolished. The shrine was then established on the site, taking the mountain name of Nanrinji. It became a village shrine, and in [[Taisho]] 9 (1920), on the 350th anniversary of the deity's death, it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. Even today, it is bustling with many worshippers during festivals and the New Year holidays, located near the busy downtown area of Kagoshima.

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Matsubara Shrine | image = [[File:Matsubara-shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Kagoshima Prefecture]][[Kagoshima City]][[Matsubara-cho (Kagoshima City)|Matsubara-cho]] 3-35 | ISO = JP-46 | latitude_deg = 31 | type_min = 35 | type_sec = 11.2 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 33 | longitude_sec = 26.8 | deity = Yasukuni Sukunehiko no Mikoto (Shimazu Takahisa) | shrine_rank = Former Prefectural Shrine | founded = Meiji 2 (1869) | main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | other_names = Daitsusa | annual_festivals = April 5, October 5 | map = Japan Kagoshima city central#Japan Kagoshima city }}
'''Matsubara Shrine''' (松原神社, Matsubara Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Matsubara-cho (Kagoshima City)|Matsubara-cho]], [[Kagoshima City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.[[ja:松原神社 (鹿児島市)]]
== Deities == The main deity is Yasukuni Sukunehiko no Mikoto, also known as [[Shimazu Takahisa]], the 15th head of the [[Shimazu clan]]. The shrine also enshrines the following deities in the same hall: * [[Amenominakanushi]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]] * [[Hirata Sumitada]]
; Tooth God Hirata Sumitada, a retainer of the main deity, is also enshrined. After the main deity's death, Hirata Sumitada became a nail-bound martyr. During his martyrdom, chanting and tooth-grinding sounds were heard for three days and nights, leading to his veneration as the god of teeth. He was buried next to the main deity. Even today, during the [[National Cavity Prevention Day]], the Kagoshima Dental Association holds an exodontia memorial service at the shrine.
== History == {{Seealso|Nanrinji (Kagoshima City)}} In [[Koji (Japan)|Koji]] 3 (1567), [[Shimazu Takahisa]], the reviver of the Shimazu clan, established Matsubarayama Nanrinji, a branch temple of [[Fukushoji]]. After the deity's death, [[Shimazu Yoshihisa]] designated it as the family temple, receiving significant protection from successive [[Satsuma Domain]] lords. However, in Meiji 2 (1869), during the [[Haibutsu Kishaku]] movement, the temple was abolished. The shrine was then established on the site, taking the mountain name of Nanrinji. It became a village shrine, and in [[Taisho]] 9 (1920), on the 350th anniversary of the deity's death, it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. Even today, it is bustling with many worshippers during festivals and the New Year holidays, located near the busy downtown area of Kagoshima.
== Sub-Shrines == * [[Inari Shrine]] ([[Inari Okami]]) * [[Itsukushima Shrine]] ([[Ichikishimahime no Mikoto]], [[Tagorihime]], [[Tagitsuhime no Mikoto]])
== References == * Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency, "Kagoshima Calendar," Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== External Links == * [https://matsubarajinja.com Matsubara Shrine Official Website] * {{wayback|url=http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-907.html |title=Matsubara Shrine |date=2015-01-06}} (Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency)

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Matsubara Shrine | image = [[File:Matsubara-shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Kagoshima Prefecture]][[Kagoshima City]][[Matsubara-cho (Kagoshima City)|Matsubara-cho]] 3-35 | ISO = JP-46 | latitude_deg = 31 | type_min = 35 | type_sec = 11.2 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 33 | longitude_sec = 26.8 | deity = Yasukuni Sukunehiko no Mikoto (Shimazu Takahisa) | shrine_rank = Former Prefectural Shrine | founded = Meiji 2 (1869) | main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | other_names = Daitsusa | annual_festivals = April 5, October 5 | map = Japan Kagoshima city central#Japan Kagoshima city }}
'''Matsubara Shrine''' (松原神社, Matsubara Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Matsubara-cho (Kagoshima City)|Matsubara-cho]], [[Kagoshima City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == The main deity is Yasukuni Sukunehiko no Mikoto, also known as [[Shimazu Takahisa]], the 15th head of the [[Shimazu clan]]. The shrine also enshrines the following deities in the same hall: * [[Amenominakanushi]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]] * [[Hirata Sumitada]]
; Tooth God Hirata Sumitada, a retainer of the main deity, is also enshrined. After the main deity's death, Hirata Sumitada became a nail-bound martyr. During his martyrdom, chanting and tooth-grinding sounds were heard for three days and nights, leading to his veneration as the god of teeth. He was buried next to the main deity. Even today, during the [[National Cavity Prevention Day]], the Kagoshima Dental Association holds an exodontia memorial service at the shrine.
== History == {{Seealso|Nanrinji (Kagoshima City)}} In [[Koji (Japan)|Koji]] 3 (1567), [[Shimazu Takahisa]], the reviver of the Shimazu clan, established Matsubarayama Nanrinji, a branch temple of [[Fukushoji]]. After the deity's death, [[Shimazu Yoshihisa]] designated it as the family temple, receiving significant protection from successive [[Satsuma Domain]] lords. However, in Meiji 2 (1869), during the [[Haibutsu Kishaku]] movement, the temple was abolished. The shrine was then established on the site, taking the mountain name of Nanrinji. It became a village shrine, and in [[Taisho]] 9 (1920), on the 350th anniversary of the deity's death, it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. Even today, it is bustling with many worshippers during festivals and the New Year holidays, located near the busy downtown area of Kagoshima.
== Sub-Shrines == * [[Inari Shrine]] ([[Inari Okami]]) * [[Itsukushima Shrine]] ([[Ichikishimahime no Mikoto]], [[Tagorihime]], [[Tagitsuhime no Mikoto]])
== References == * Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency, "Kagoshima Calendar," Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== External Links == * [https://matsubarajinja.com Matsubara Shrine Official Website] * {{wayback|url=http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-907.html |title=Matsubara Shrine |date=2015-01-06}} (Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency)

== History == {{Seealso|Nanrinji (Kagoshima City)}} In [[Koji (Japan)|Koji]] 3 (1567), [[Shimazu Takahisa]], the reviver of the Shimazu clan, established Matsubarayama Nanrinji, a branch temple of [[Fukushoji]]. After the deity's death, [[Shimazu Yoshihisa]] designated it as the family temple, receiving significant protection from successive [[Satsuma Domain]] lords. However, in Meiji 2 (1869), during the [[Haibutsu Kishaku]] movement, the temple was abolished. The shrine was then established on the site, taking the mountain name of Nanrinji. It became a village shrine, and in [[Taisho]] 9 (1920), on the 350th anniversary of the deity's death, it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. Even today, it is bustling with many worshippers during festivals and the New Year holidays, located near the busy downtown area of Kagoshima.

== Deities == The main deity is Yasukuni Sukunehiko no Mikoto, also known as [[Shimazu Takahisa]], the 15th head of the [[Shimazu clan]]. The shrine also enshrines the following deities in the same hall: * [[Amenominakanushi]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]] * [[Hirata Sumitada]]

During his martyrdom, chanting and tooth-grinding sounds were heard for three days and nights, leading to his veneration as the god of teeth. He was buried next to the main deity. Even today, during the [[National Cavity Prevention Day]], the Kagoshima Dental Association holds an exodontia memorial service at the shrine.

'''Matsubara Shrine''' (松原神社, Matsubara Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Matsubara-cho (Kagoshima City)|Matsubara-cho]], [[:en:Kagoshima City|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == The main deity is Yasukuni Sukunehiko no Mikoto, also known as [[:en:Shimazu Takahisa|Shimazu Takahisa]], the 15th head of the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]. The shrine also enshrines the following deities in the same hall: * [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]] * [[Hirata Sumitada]]
; Tooth God Hirata Sumitada, a retainer of the main deity, is also enshrined. After the main deity's death, Hirata Sumitada became a nail-bound martyr. During his martyrdom, chanting and tooth-grinding sounds were heard for three days and nights, leading to his veneration as the god of teeth. He was buried next to the main deity. Even today, during the [[National Cavity Prevention Day]], the Kagoshima Dental Association holds an exodontia memorial service at the shrine.
== History == {{Seealso|Nanrinji (Kagoshima City)}} In [[Koji (Japan)|Koji]] 3 (1567), [[:en:Shimazu Takahisa|Shimazu Takahisa]], the reviver of the Shimazu clan, established Matsubarayama Nanrinji, a branch temple of [[Fukushoji]]. After the deity's death, [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]] designated it as the family temple, receiving significant protection from successive [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] lords. However, in Meiji 2 (1869), during the [[Haibutsu Kishaku]] movement, the temple was abolished. The shrine was then established on the site, taking the mountain name of Nanrinji. It became a village shrine, and in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 9 (1920), on the 350th anniversary of the deity's death, it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. Even today, it is bustling with many worshippers during festivals and the New Year holidays, located near the busy downtown area of Kagoshima.
== Sub-Shrines == * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrine]] ([[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * [[Itsukushima Shrine]] ([[Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto]], [[Tagorihime]], [[Tagitsuhime|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto]])
== References == * Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency, "Kagoshima Calendar," Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== External Links == * [https://matsubarajinja.com Matsubara Shrine Official Website] * {{wayback|url=http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-907.html |title=Matsubara Shrine |date=2015-01-06}} (Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency)

'''Matsubara Shrine''' (松原神社, Matsubara Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Matsubara-cho (Kagoshima City)|Matsubara-cho]], [[:en:Kagoshima City|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == The main deity is Yasukuni Sukunehiko no Mikoto, also known as [[:en:Shimazu Takahisa|Shimazu Takahisa]], the 15th head of the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]. The shrine also enshrines the following deities in the same hall: * [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]] * [[Hirata Sumitada]]
; Tooth God Hirata Sumitada, a retainer of the main deity, is also enshrined. After the main deity's death, Hirata Sumitada became a nail-bound martyr. During his martyrdom, chanting and tooth-grinding sounds were heard for three days and nights, leading to his veneration as the god of teeth. He was buried next to the main deity. Even today, during the [[National Cavity Prevention Day]], the Kagoshima Dental Association holds an exodontia memorial service at the shrine.
== History == {{Seealso|Nanrinji (Kagoshima City)}} In [[Koji (Japan)|Koji]] 3 (1567), [[:en:Shimazu Takahisa|Shimazu Takahisa]], the reviver of the Shimazu clan, established Matsubarayama Nanrinji, a branch temple of [[Fukushoji]]. After the deity's death, [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]] designated it as the family temple, receiving significant protection from successive [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] lords. However, in Meiji 2 (1869), during the [[Haibutsu Kishaku]] movement, the temple was abolished. The shrine was then established on the site, taking the mountain name of Nanrinji. It became a village shrine, and in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 9 (1920), on the 350th anniversary of the deity's death, it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. Even today, it is bustling with many worshippers during festivals and the New Year holidays, located near the busy downtown area of Kagoshima.
== Sub-Shrines == * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrine]] ([[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * [[Itsukushima Shrine]] ([[Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto]], [[Tagorihime]], [[Tagitsuhime|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto]])
== References == * Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency, "Kagoshima Calendar," Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== External Links == * [https://matsubarajinja.com Matsubara Shrine Official Website] * {{wayback|url=http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-907.html |title=Matsubara Shrine |date=2015-01-06}} (Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency)

== History == {{Seealso|Nanrinji (Kagoshima City)}} In [[Koji (Japan)|Koji]] 3 (1567), [[:en:Shimazu Takahisa|Shimazu Takahisa]], the reviver of the Shimazu clan, established Matsubarayama Nanrinji, a branch temple of [[Fukushoji]]. After the deity's death, [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]] designated it as the family temple, receiving significant protection from successive [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] lords. However, in Meiji 2 (1869), during the [[Haibutsu Kishaku]] movement, the temple was abolished. The shrine was then established on the site, taking the mountain name of Nanrinji. It became a village shrine, and in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 9 (1920), on the 350th anniversary of the deity's death, it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. Even today, it is bustling with many worshippers during festivals and the New Year holidays, located near the busy downtown area of Kagoshima.

== History == {{Seealso|Nanrinji (Kagoshima City)}} In [[Koji (Japan)|Koji]] 3 (1567), {{ill|Shimazu Takahisa|en|Shimazu Takahisa|WD=Q2367308|lt=Shimazu Takahisa}}, the reviver of the Shimazu clan, established Matsubarayama Nanrinji, a branch temple of [[Fukushoji]]. After the deity's death, {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|en|Shimazu Yoshihisa|WD=Q955727|lt=Shimazu Yoshihisa}} designated it as the family temple, receiving significant protection from successive {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain}} lords. However, in Meiji 2 (1869), during the [[Haibutsu Kishaku]] movement, the temple was abolished. The shrine was then established on the site, taking the mountain name of Nanrinji. It became a village shrine, and in {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era}} 9 (1920), on the 350th anniversary of the deity's death, it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. Even today, it is bustling with many worshippers during festivals and the New Year holidays, located near the busy downtown area of Kagoshima.
== Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine}} ({{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Ōkami}}) * [[Itsukushima Shrine]] ([[Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto]], [[Tagorihime]], [[Tagitsuhime|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto]])

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Matsubara Shrine | image = File:Matsubara-shrine.jpg | location = {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}[[:en:Kagoshima City|Kagoshima City]][[Matsubara-cho (Kagoshima City)|Matsubara-cho]] 3-35 | ISO = JP-46 | latitude_deg = 31 | type_min = 35 | type_sec = 11.2 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 33 | longitude_sec = 26.8 | deity = {{ill|Shimazu Takahisa|WD=Q2367308|1=Shimazu Takahisa}} | shrine_Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine | founded = Meiji 2 (1869) | main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | other_names = Daitsusa | annual_festivals = April 5, October 5 | map = Japan Kagoshima city central#Japan Kagoshima city | prefecture= {{ill|Matsubara-chō|WD=Q11529436|1=Matsubara-chō}} | coordinates= {{Coord|31.586444444444446|N|130.55744444444446|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }}
'''Matsubara Shrine''' (松原神社, Matsubara Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Matsubara-cho (Kagoshima City)|Matsubara-cho]], [[:en:Kagoshima City|Kagoshima City]], {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Deities == The main deity is Yasukuni Sukunehiko no Mikoto, also known as {{ill|Shimazu Takahisa|en|Shimazu Takahisa|WD=Q2367308|lt=Shimazu Takahisa|1=Shimazu Takahisa}}, the 15th head of the {{ill|Shimazu clan|en|Shimazu clan|WD=Q1061406|lt=Shimazu clan|1=Shimazu clan}}. The shrine also enshrines the following deities in the same hall: * [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * [[Amaterasu]] * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Okami|1=Toyouke-hime}} * [[Hirata Sumitada]]
; Tooth God Hirata Sumitada, a retainer of the main deity, is also enshrined. After the main deity's death, Hirata Sumitada became a nail-bound martyr. During his martyrdom, chanting and tooth-grinding sounds were heard for three days and nights, leading to his veneration as the god of teeth. He was buried next to the main deity. Even today, during the [[National Cavity Prevention Day]], the Kagoshima Dental Association holds an exodontia memorial service at the shrine.
== History == {{Seealso|Nanrinji (Kagoshima City)}} In [[Koji (Japan)|Koji]] 3 (1567), {{ill|Shimazu Takahisa|en|Shimazu Takahisa|WD=Q2367308|lt=Shimazu Takahisa|1=Shimazu Takahisa}}, the reviver of the Shimazu clan, established Matsubarayama Nanrinji, a branch temple of [[Fukushoji]]. After the deity's death, {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|en|Shimazu Yoshihisa|WD=Q955727|lt=Shimazu Yoshihisa|1=Shimazu Yoshihisa}} designated it as the family temple, receiving significant protection from successive {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain|1=Satsuma Domain}} lords. However, in Meiji 2 (1869), during the [[Haibutsu Kishaku]] movement, the temple was abolished. The shrine was then established on the site, taking the mountain name of Nanrinji. It became a village shrine, and in {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 9 (1920), on the 350th anniversary of the deity's death, it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. Even today, it is bustling with many worshippers during festivals and the New Year holidays, located near the busy downtown area of Kagoshima.
== Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine|1=Inari shrine}} ({{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Ōkami|1=Inari Ōkami}}) * [[Itsukushima Shrine]] ([[Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto]], [[Tagorihime]], [[Tagitsuhime|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto]])
== References == * Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency, "Kagoshima Calendar," Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency.
== External Links == * [https://matsubarajinja.com Matsubara Shrine Official Website] * {{wayback|url=http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-907.html |title=Matsubara Shrine |date=2015-01-06}} (Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Agency)
